item.title: From copper imbalance to immunometabolic remodeling: cuproptosis-related vulnerability and therapeutic hypotheses in autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Review - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) are characterized by disrupted immune tolerance, chronic inflammation, and organ-specific tissue injury. Their pathogenesis involves the interplay between genetic suscept...

item.title: SHIP1 is required for T cell surveillance of occult malignancies.
item.description: Cellular and molecular life sciences : CMLS - SHIP1-deficient mice develop severe, transmural Crohn's-like ileitis and die prematurely. Recent analysis of a human Crohn's disease (CD) cohort revealed a significant subset of patients have sever...

item.title: Inflammasomes in Intestinal Inflammation: Guardians of Mucosal Homeostasis, Amplifiers of Disease, and Targets for Intervention.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - The intestinal mucosa is continuously exposed to dietary antigens, commensal microorganisms, and enteric pathogens, thereby depending on innate immune pathways that detect danger without compromisi...

item.title: Autoimmune diseases in the era of COVID-19: emerging mechanisms, clinical implications, vaccine considerations, and future directions.
item.description: The Korean journal of internal medicine - Review - The coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic has highlighted a complex, bidirectional relationship between SARSCoV-2 infection and autoimmune diseases. This review examines how SARS-CoV-2 infec...

item.title: Lymphomatous transformation in mass-forming chronic active EBV-associated enteritis initially misdiagnosed as ulcerative colitis: A case report.
item.description: Medicine - Case report - Chronic active Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) disease is a group of refractory and progressive lymphoproliferative disorders characterized by both inflammatory and clonal proliferative features. Gastroin...

item.title: Clinical isolates of Fusobacterium nucleatum display strain-specific virulence and modulation by indole derivatives.
item.description: Microbiology (Reading, England) - Pathogenic bacteria adapt to distinct disease environments, but whether these adaptations create therapeutic vulnerabilities remains unclear. Fusobacterium nucleatum has emerged as a key microbial ...

item.title: Immune cell-derived membrane nanovesicles: A promethean fire for autoimmune disease therapy through immune cell mimicry.
item.description: Bioactive materials - Review - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) constitute a heterogeneous group of disorders characterized by immune dysregulation, loss of self-tolerance, and chronic inflammation, which leads to tissue damage and or...
